title: US major indices close higher for the 2nd consecutive day. Gains led by small-cap stocks.
description: U.S. stocks closed higher, with broad-based gains across the major indices signaling a constructive tone into the end of the session. The small-cap Russell 2000 led the gains with a gain of 0.67%. The rise is indicative of investors willingness to take on riskier assets. The NASDAQ led the major index move move, rising 0.47%, as technology shares continued to benefit from renewed interest in AI-driven growth themes. The S&P 500 gained 0.25%, holding near recent highs, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average lagged slightly but still advanced 0.10%. The overall price action suggests a market that is grinding higher rather than surging, with buyers maintaining control but without aggressive momentum. Under the surface, the tone reflects improving sentiment and selective rotation into growth and smaller-cap names, even as traders remain mindful of macro risks and upcoming catalysts. This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.

title: New HEAL Collaborative Analysis Reveals 340B Hospitals Increase Hospital Assets While Reducing Patient Care Spending
description: WASHINGTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--A new state-by-state analysis released today by HEAL Collaborative provides further insight into how hospitals participating in the 340B program are allocating resources. The study, titled "The 340B Drug Pricing Program by State: How Covered Entities Are Failing to Invest in Patient Care," was conducted by Magnolia Market Access and provides the first detailed look at how hospitals participating in the federal 340B drug discount program are utilizing program revenue

description: The tournament brings nothing but positives for Nevada, which opens Wednesday at home against Murray State.

title: Beloved Philadelphia restaurant to close after nearly 70 years
description: The family-run establishment, which has been around for four generations, will close its doors as the owners prepare for retirement.

title: Activists push back against CU decision to keep Key Lime Air
description: The University of Colorado’s Board of Regents' decision not to reconsider its partnership with Key Lime Air, which charters flights for Immigration and Customs Enforcement detainees, is under scrutiny. On Tuesday, activists decried the move at a news conference and rally in front of the CU offices in downtown Denver.

title: Meghan Markle's hairdresser George Northwood, 47, announces the birth of his surrogate son, Elijah
description: Meghan Markle's hairdresser George Northwood has announced the birth of his surrogate son, Elijah.
